title: harmsia lepidota
intro: Harmsia lepidota, also known as the West African Harmal or Yellow-Bellied Harmal, is a plant species within the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant's seeds contain a high concentration of the alkaloid harmine, which has been found to exhibit medicinal properties, including antibacterial and antiviral properties. Harmsia lepidota is believed to provide various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and other conditions. Herbal preparations of Harmsia lepidota are often available in capsule or tincture form, and are sometimes used in combination with other herbal remedies to enhance their effects.
